figures.caption: Table 1: Performance of ROAD and the baseline methods for offline data replay under Antmaze tasks, Locomotion tasks and Kitchen tasks with IQL. All results are assessed across 4 random seeds. The best performance for fixed mixing ratios is underlined, and the best-performed score is bolded.

Figure 2: Heatmap for mixing ratio selection in ROAD. In some cases, ROAD could learn to converge to the mixing ratio that performs best when fixed (left three), while in some other cases it could adjust its mixing ratio pattern more adaptively (right two).

Figure 4: An example of offline-to-online RL with various dataset qualities and the evaluated policy quality. Left: Illustrates a simple MDP with an example of sub-optimal/optimal policies. An agent initiates from the start state S, with options to advance either 1 or 2 steps towards the terminal state T in each timestep. Right: Demonstrates the average return and perceived quality of the current policy by the agent against offline and online data under mixing ratios of 0.1 and 0.5 with lower-quality offline data generated with sub-optimal policy (upper) and higher-quality offline data generated with an equal blend of sub-optimal policy and optimal policy (lower).
Figure 6: Normalized Returns of ROAD and the baseline methods with Cal-QL [Nakamoto et al., 2023] as the backbone in Antmaze and Adroit tasks.
Figure 7: Normalized Returns of ROAD and the baseline methods with CQL [Kumar et al., 2020] as the backbone in Antmaze and Adroit tasks.
Figure 8: Normalized Returns of ROAD and the fixed mixing ratio approaches with RLPD [Kumar et al., 2020] as the backbone in Antmaze, Locomotion and Adroit tasks.

Table 4: Performance of ROAD and the baseline methods for offline data replay under Antmaze tasks, Locomotion tasks and Kitchen tasks with PEX. All results are assessed across 4 random seeds. The best performance for fixed mixing ratios is underlined, and the best-performed score is bolded.
Table 6: Main hyper-parameters for IQL, PEX, CQL and Cal-QL in ROAD.
Figure 9: Examining ROAD under the setting of online RL with offline data without offline pretraining with various dataset qualities.

Figure 1: Illustration of the online training scheme of ROAD. In the online training phase, ROAD controls the offline data replay pattern by selecting the mixing ratio in an adaptive fashion with the derived surrogate objective for the bi-level optimization problem. The mixing ratio m adjusted by ROAD controls the mixed replay for the offline dataset and the online buffer.
Figure 5: A selection of benchmark tasks in Antmaze Task Domain (left), Locomotion Task Domain (middle), and Adroit Task Domain (right).
